meta.title: Protestantism vs Catholicism Explained | Protesting or Catholic
meta.author: Canon Press

questions.question: According to the outline, what are the two equally authoritative parts of the Roman Catholic "deposit of faith"?
questions.answer: Scripture and tradition

questions.question: What does Sola Scriptura mean for historic Protestants?
options: Scripture is one of several authorities
Scripture alone is the infallible rule for faith and practice
Scripture must be read through church tradition
Scripture is equal to papal pronouncements
questions.answer: Scripture alone is the infallible rule for faith and practice

questions.question: Why does the outline say Protestant objections to Catholicism center on "accretions"?
options: Because Catholics added too many feast days
Because most distinguishing Catholic traditions evolved over centuries and are not apostolic or biblical
Because the Pope added new books to the Bible
Because Catholic churches are older than Protestant ones
questions.answer: Because most distinguishing Catholic traditions evolved over centuries and are not apostolic or biblical

questions.question: Which event is identified as the famous spark of the Reformation?
options: Martin Luther's 95 Theses at Wittenberg in 1517
The publication of the Gutenberg Bible
questions.answer: Martin Luther's 95 Theses at Wittenberg in 1517

questions.question: What is the magisterium in Roman Catholicism?
options: The teaching office of the Roman church (councils and papal pronouncements) that guards and interprets the deposit of faith
The collection of all papal encyclicals
questions.answer: The teaching office of the Roman church (councils and papal pronouncements) that guards and interprets the deposit of faith

questions.question: According to the outline, what do historic Protestants claim about their position—innovation or restoration?
options: Restoration (ad fontes) to scripture and the early church approach
A compromise between Rome and the Eastern church
A new religion separate from Christianity
questions.answer: Restoration (ad fontes) to scripture and the early church approach

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as one of the four central Protestant objections to Roman Catholicism?
options: The cult of Mary and Marian dogmas
The use of Latin in the Mass
Justification by faith and the Roman teaching on grace
questions.answer: The use of Latin in the Mass

questions.question: What does the outline say about the papacy and scripture?
options: The papacy is clearly taught in Matthew 16
The papacy is not expressly taught in scripture, and Vatican I's claim is contrary to that
Scripture is silent on church government
The papacy is taught in the book of Revelation
questions.answer: The papacy is not expressly taught in scripture, and Vatican I's claim is contrary to that

questions.question: What are indulgences, as discussed in the material?
options: Remissions of temporal punishment for sin, often linked to payment; a catalyst for the Reformation
questions.answer: Remissions of temporal punishment for sin, often linked to payment; a catalyst for the Reformation

questions.question: Which two main Protestant traditions emerged from the Reformation?
questions.answer: Lutheran and Reformed

questions.question: What does Solus Christus affirm?
options: Christ is present in the Eucharist
The work of Christ alone is the basis on which sinners are justified before God
Christ gave the keys to all the apostles
questions.answer: The work of Christ alone is the basis on which sinners are justified before God

questions.question: According to the outline, why is "tradition" a problem for Protestant critics of Rome?
options: Tradition is never mentioned in the Bible
Tradition and magisterial authority are extremely undefined and inconsistent; tradition includes forgeries and morally troubling teachings now ignored
questions.answer: Tradition and magisterial authority are extremely undefined and inconsistent; tradition includes forgeries and morally troubling teachings now ignored

questions.question: Cardinal John Henry Newman is cited in the outline for which saying?
options: "To be deep in history is to cease to be Protestant"
questions.answer: "To be deep in history is to cease to be Protestant"

questions.question: How does the outline characterize the contrast between Protestant and Roman views of church history?
options: Ancient sources versus ancient sources plus accumulated medieval tradition
questions.answer: Ancient sources versus ancient sources plus accumulated medieval tradition

questions.question: What does the outline say the Council of Trent teaches about the magisterium and scripture?
options: Scripture may be read in the vernacular
No one ought to dare contradict the magisterium's interpretation of scripture
questions.answer: No one ought to dare contradict the magisterium's interpretation of scripture

questions.question: What is the title Theotokos used to mean?
options: "God-bearer"; title for Mary as mother of God
questions.answer: "God-bearer"; title for Mary as mother of God

questions.question: According to the outline, why do Protestants not see denominational diversity as undermining their position?
options: Because they have a global pope
Because they do not view their churches as the one true church but as part of the universal church, and scripture remains the common ultimate authority
Because all denominations agree on every doctrine
Because diversity was approved at Nicaea
questions.answer: Because they do not view their churches as the one true church but as part of the universal church, and scripture remains the common ultimate authority

questions.question: Which papal bull is cited in connection with authorization of conquest and subjugation of infidels?
questions.answer: Dum diversas

questions.question: What does the outline say the Roman church believes about the Pope and apostolic authority?
options: The Pope is first among equals
The Pope is Jesus Christ's visible representative on earth and wields apostolic authority, succeeding from Peter
The Pope is elected by the laity
The Pope cannot err in matters of doctrine
questions.answer: The Pope is Jesus Christ's visible representative on earth and wields apostolic authority, succeeding from Peter

questions.question: Sola Fide refers to what?
options: Justification in Christ received by faith alone
questions.answer: Justification in Christ received by faith alone

questions.question: According to the outline, what did the Reformation shift away from?
options: Accumulated tradition, on the basis of scripture alone
questions.answer: Accumulated tradition, on the basis of scripture alone

questions.question: Why does the outline say a teaching that appears in the fifth century is "late"?
options: Because the fifth century had bad theologians
Because hundreds of years separate it from the apostles; errors could have crept in
Because the canon was not yet closed
Because Rome had not yet defined doctrine
questions.answer: Because hundreds of years separate it from the apostles; errors could have crept in

questions.question: What is transubstantiation?
options: The belief that Christ is spiritually present in the Lord's Supper
The doctrine that the bread and wine of the Eucharist become the body and blood of Christ in substance while retaining the appearance of bread and wine
The transformation of the priest during Mass
The conversion of sinners to the faith
questions.answer: The doctrine that the bread and wine of the Eucharist become the body and blood of Christ in substance while retaining the appearance of bread and wine

questions.question: Which nations or regions are mentioned as having broken with Rome and become Protestant?
options: Scotland, England, the Netherlands, Scandinavia, and German principalities
questions.answer: Scotland, England, the Netherlands, Scandinavia, and German principalities

questions.question: What does the outline conclude about being "truly deep in history"?
options: It is to be Pauline and Protestant—clinging to the pure word of God and rejecting accumulated errors
questions.answer: It is to be Pauline and Protestant—clinging to the pure word of God and rejecting accumulated errors

questions.question: According to the outline, what is the common bond uniting Protestant denominations?
options: Repudiation of centralized Roman authority and infallible tradition
questions.answer: Repudiation of centralized Roman authority and infallible tradition

questions.question: What does ad fontes mean?
options: "To the sources"; return to original texts (e.g., scripture and early church) rather than medieval tradition
questions.answer: "To the sources"; return to original texts (e.g., scripture and early church) rather than medieval tradition

questions.question: Why do Lutherans often restrict communion to Lutherans, according to the outline?
options: Because of their unique understanding of Christ's presence in the Eucharist
Because of the Council of Trent
questions.answer: Because of their unique understanding of Christ's presence in the Eucharist

questions.question: What does the outline say the papacy is sustained by more than by historical or biblical evidence?
questions.answer: Institutional heft and power
